name: Jest
what_it_does: JavaScript and TypeScript test runner with built-in mocking, snapshot testing, and code coverage for unit and integration tests.
use_cases: Generate unit tests for JavaScript/TypeScript modules using jest.fn() and jest.spyOn() to isolate dependencies
Write snapshot tests for React components to catch unintended UI regressions across code changes
Mock entire Node.js modules via jest.mock() to test code that depends on file system, databases, or external APIs
Use fake timers (jest.useFakeTimers) to test debounce, throttle, setTimeout, and setInterval logic deterministically
Filter and run specific test subsets with --testNamePattern or --testPathPattern to speed up feedback loops during development
not_for: Browser E2E testing — Jest runs in jsdom (not a real browser) and cannot test actual rendering pipelines
Python, Ruby, or other non-JS/TS ecosystems where language-native frameworks like pytest are more appropriate
Load or performance testing — Jest is designed for correctness, not concurrency or throughput measurement
best_when: Testing JavaScript or TypeScript business logic, utility functions, and React component behavior with rich mocking and snapshot support.
avoid_when: You need real browser behavior or are testing non-JavaScript code.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers for Jest itself

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Jest diff output clearly shows expected vs received values with colored diffs; snapshot mismatch errors include line-level diff.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tests are deterministic when app state is properly reset; jest.clearAllMocks() or jest.resetAllMocks() in afterEach prevents mock state leakage.
known_agent_gotchas: jest.mock() calls are hoisted to the top of the file by Babel transform — agents must not place logic that depends on runtime values inside jest.mock() factory functions

Mixing ESM and CommonJS modules causes 'Cannot use import statement' or 'require is not defined' errors; agents must configure transform and extensionsToTreatAsEsm correctly for the project's module system
jest.spyOn() on an imported function only works if the module exports an object — spying on named exports from ES modules requires mocking the whole module first
Snapshot files must be committed to version control; agents that run tests in CI without committing __snapshots__ directories will see failing snapshot tests on first run
Fake timers do not advance automatically — agents must call jest.runAllTimers() or jest.advanceTimersByTime(N) explicitly to trigger deferred callbacks

security_score_components.security_notes: Secrets needed by tests should be loaded from environment variables via process.env, never hardcoded; jest.config.js should not contain credentials.
